title: Frontier AI goes local as big-tech's grip slips - TCR 05/31/26
description:
Open-weight AI models that match proprietary systems now run offline on a $400 laptop with refusal layers stripped in minutes across 6,000-plus abliterated copies, a triple-action injection called amivantamab erased entire tumours in 15 of 102 head-and-neck cancer patients at ASCO, and SoftBank committed up to €75 billion to build 5 gigawatts of data-center capacity in France.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-31-2026/

0:00 AI companion robots in Australian aged care
5:27 Amivantamab and daraxonrasib cancer readouts at ASCO
8:08 SoftBank's €75B France compute and European sovereignty
10:21 Pangram detector misfires and synthetic influencers
13:51 The Train to the Swiss Alps That Never Leaves the Building
16:09 Close

This episode covers the open-weight frontier models and Heretic abliteration, the amivantamab and daraxonrasib ASCO cancer readouts, DARPA's RAPIID synthetic-blood program, SoftBank's France compute commitment and Mistral's Louvre summit, the Pangram AI detector misfires and synthetic TikTok influencers, and autonomous AI agents renegotiating who acts.

The headlining story is the largest single redistribution of raw capability to ordinary people that has ever happened. Frontier-tier intelligence that lived behind a handful of corporate logins is now downloadable, runnable on cheap hardware, and answerable to no platform. The panic in the press is real and belongs in the story as a claim, made loudest by the people who just lost their exclusive hold. Underneath it, medicine compressed from both ends as amivantamab demonstrated complete tumour eradication in patients who had run out of options and daraxonrasib reached patients ahead of approval. Europe wrote its claim to sovereign compute in euros, companion robots entered Australian aged care, and the authentication layer cracked in both directions - a trusted detector misfiring on real human writing while synthetic faces manufactured trust to sell dropshipped junk.

Additional Reading:
- NPR: These AI Models Are Free, Private, and Will Never Say 'No': https://www.npr.org/2026/05/31/nx-s1-5816391/ai-safety-concerns-danger-open-weight-models-risks

- TechCrunch: SoftBank to Invest Up to €75 Billion to Build French Data Centers: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/30/softbank-says-it-will-invest-up-to-e75-billion-to-build-french-data-centers/
- The Atlantic: America Has a Pangram Problem: https://www.theatlantic.com/technology/2026/05/pangram-ai-detection-accuracy/687381/
